Question: Explain the nature of light.
Answer: Light is a form of energy that travels in straight lines
column5: and enables us to see objects. It is a visible portion of the electromagnetic spectrum.

Question: Describe how shadows are formed.
Answer: Shadows form when an opaque object blocks light. The area behind the object receives less or no light

Question: Explain the difference between transparent
Answer: translucent
column4: Transparent objects allow light to pass completely (glass)

Question: Describe the laws of reflection.
Answer: 1) The angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection. 2) Incident ray
column4: and normal lie in the same plane.

Question: Explain how plane mirrors form images.
Answer: Plane mirrors reflect light in a regular pattern
column5: same-size images at the same distance behind the mirror as the object.

Question: Describe the difference between real and virtual images.
Answer: Real images can be projected on a screen
column3: formed by converging rays; virtual images cannot be projected
column4: appear behind the mirror or lens (plane mirror image).

Question: Explain how concave mirrors form images with examples.
Answer: Concave mirrors converge light. If object is beyond focus
column3: image is real and inverted; if between focus and mirror
column4: image is virtual and magnified. Example: shaving mirror.

Question: Describe how convex mirrors form images.
Answer: Convex mirrors diverge light
column5: and diminished images. Used in vehicle rear-view mirrors.

Question: Explain how the size and position of shadows change.
Answer: Shadow size increases when object moves closer to screen
column3: decreases with more distance. Sunlight angle also affects shape and length.

Question: Describe the concept of reflection of light on smooth and rough surfaces.
Answer: Smooth surfaces reflect light regularly (specular reflection)
column3: rough surfaces scatter light in all directions (diffuse reflection).

Question: Explain how pinhole cameras work.
Answer: A pinhole camera forms inverted real images of objects on a screen through a small aperture

Question: Describe the process of formation of rainbow.
Answer: Sunlight is refracted

Question: Explain the difference between regular and diffused reflection.
Answer: Regular reflection occurs on smooth surfaces with clear image; diffused reflection occurs on rough surfaces scattering light

Question: Describe the effect of distance of light source on shadow size.
Answer: Closer light source produces larger shadow
column3: farther source produces smaller shadow due to divergence of light rays.

Question: Explain how lenses bend light.
Answer: Convex lenses converge light to a focus; concave lenses diverge light. Refraction at lens surfaces changes light direction.

Question: Describe how image formation by convex and concave lenses differs.
Answer: Convex lens can produce real
column4: magnified images depending on object distance; concave lens always forms virtual

Question: Explain the phenomenon of dispersion of light.
Answer: Dispersion occurs when white light splits into its component colors passing through a prism
column3: due to different bending of wavelengths.

Question: Describe how mirrors are used in daily life.
Answer: Mirrors are used for reflection (personal grooming)

Question: Explain the principle of periscope operation.
Answer: Periscope uses two plane mirrors at 45° to reflect light

Question: Describe how total internal reflection is applied.
Answer: Total internal reflection occurs when light moves from denser to rarer medium beyond critical angle. Used in optical fibers and binoculars.

Question: Explain how refractive index is related to bending of light.
Answer: Refractive index measures how much light bends entering a medium; higher refractive index bends light more.

Question: Describe how shadows can be used to tell time.
Answer: Sundials use the position and length of shadows cast by gnomon (stick) in sunlight to indicate time based on sun’s movement.

Question: Explain how image orientation changes with mirrors.
Answer: Plane mirrors produce upright images; concave/convex mirrors and lenses can produce inverted or magnified images depending on object position.

Question: Describe the role of light in seeing objects.
Answer: Objects are seen when they reflect or emit light that enters our eyes and stimulates retina
column3: forming images interpreted by the brain.

Question: Explain the difference between partial and total eclipse.
Answer: Partial eclipse – only part of sun or moon is obscured; total eclipse – completely covered by another celestial body.

Question: Describe how light enables color perception.
Answer: Objects absorb certain wavelengths and reflect others; reflected light enters eyes

Question: Explain how concave mirrors are used in headlights.
Answer: Concave mirrors focus light from bulb into parallel beam

Question: Describe the effect of mirror curvature on image size.
Answer: Greater curvature of concave mirror increases magnification; convex mirrors reduce image size due to divergent reflection.

Question: Explain how light travels in straight lines with examples.
Answer: Light travels in straight paths in homogeneous medium; evidenced by shadows

Question: Describe the difference between objective and eyepiece in microscopes.
Answer: Objective lens forms real magnified image; eyepiece lens further magnifies it to be seen by observer.

Question: Explain how optical instruments use lenses and mirrors.
Answer: Telescopes
column4: and cameras use combination of lenses/mirrors to bend light

Question: Describe how rainbow colors are arranged and why.
Answer: Violet bends most
column3: red least due to dispersion; order: red

Question: Explain how light reflection helps in signaling.
Answer: Mirrors or shiny surfaces reflect light in specific directions to send signals over distance

Question: Describe the formation of shadows on Earth.
Answer: Earth blocks sunlight
column3: forming shadows; size and direction change with sun’s position (morning/evening vs noon).

Question: Explain how convex lenses are used in magnifying glasses.
Answer: Convex lenses converge light
column3: forming enlarged virtual images when object is within focal length.

Question: Describe how concave mirrors are used in shaving and makeup mirrors.
Answer: Concave mirrors produce magnified virtual images for close inspection when object is within focus.

Question: Explain how lens focal length affects image formation.
Answer: Shorter focal length produces larger magnification; longer focal length produces smaller images at greater distance.

Question: Describe the role of prisms in separating light.
Answer: Prisms refract and disperse white light into its constituent colors

Question: Explain how light enables vision in the dark with help of artificial sources.
Answer: Artificial sources emit light
column4: enabling vision in absence of sunlight.

Question: Describe the difference between real and virtual shadows.
Answer: Real shadows can be projected on screens with opaque light source; virtual shadows cannot be projected
column4: due to diffuse or multiple light sources.

Question: Explain how periscopes help submarines.
Answer: Periscopes use reflection to allow observation above water level while submarine remains submerged.

Question: Describe how multiple reflections occur in mirrors.
Answer: Light reflects multiple times between mirrors at angles
column3: producing repeated images or kaleidoscope effect.
